item.title: Australia Urged to Protect Iranian Women's Football Team After Asian Cup Elimination
item.description: Concerns for the safety of Iran's women's football team have grown after they declined to sing the national anthem ahead of their first match against South Korea. Australian authorities are being urged to protect the team as they prepare to fly home from the Asian Cup.

item.title: New Faces Revealed in Bid to Solve Reservoir Mystery
item.description: Police release new images of a man's face in a bid to solve the mystery of his death in a remote Welsh reservoir, 18 months after his body was found in a £200 XXL wetsuit.

item.title: Gerry Adams Faces Civil Court Case Over IRA Bombings
item.description: Gerry Adams faces a civil court case in London over his alleged liability for IRA bombings in 1973 and 1996. The three victims are seeking a ruling that Adams is personally responsible for the attacks.

item.title: Iranians Describe Ongoing Strikes on Oil Depots Amid US-Israeli Attack
item.description: Iranians describe the strikes on oil depots as 'night turned into day' as the US-Israeli attack on the country continues. The strikes have killed at least 1,332 Iranian civilians and wounded thousands.

item.title: Australians Must Prove Age to Access Adult Content Under New Laws
item.description: Australians must prove they are over 18 to access adult content under new laws aimed at protecting children from harmful material.

item.title: Rihanna's Beverly Hills Home Hit by Gunfire, Police Say
item.description: Rihanna's Beverly Hills home was hit by gunfire on Sunday, with a suspect taken into custody, according to the Los Angeles Police Department.

item.title: Paddington Musical Dominates WhatsOnStage Awards
item.description: Paddington The Musical has won nine WhatsOnStage Awards, the most ever for a new musical, and cemented its place as a fan-favourite in the West End.

item.title: Firefighters Battle Blaze Near Glasgow Central Station
item.description: A fire broke out near Glasgow Central Station on Sunday, causing significant disruptions to travel and raising concerns about safety in the area.

item.title: NHS England Pauses Cross-Sex Hormone Prescriptions for Under-18s
item.description: NHS England has paused new prescriptions of cross-sex hormones to 16 and 17-year-olds due to a lack of strong evidence on their benefits or harms, and has launched a 90-day consultation on longer-term guidance.

item.title: Spain Grants Amnesty to Half a Million Undocumented Migrants
item.description: Spain's government has announced a scheme to grant legal status to half a million undocumented migrants, providing them with a one-year residency visa. The move has been welcomed by migrants and the international community, who see it as a pragmatic and humanitarian measure.

item.title: Masked Fan Disrupts VAR Monitor in German Second Division Match
item.description: A masked fan unplugged a VAR monitor during a German second division match, causing a penalty to be awarded to Hertha Berlin. Preussen Munster is taking measures to prevent similar incidents.

item.title: Global Tensions Rise as Iran Threatens Oil Facilities and Announces New Supreme Leader
item.description: Iran threatens to attack oil facilities, while announcing a new Supreme Leader, and the world economy braces for potential disruption.

item.title: Two Deaths Linked to Weight-Loss Jabs in Northern Ireland
item.description: Two deaths in Northern Ireland potentially linked to weight-loss injections have been reported to the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA). The cases are among over 500 suspected adverse drug reaction reports submitted from the region over the last two years.

item.title: Clumber Spaniel Bruin Wins Best in Show at Crufts 2026
item.description: A Clumber spaniel named Bruin has won the best in show title at Crufts 2026, beating nearly 19,000 competitors. Bruin and his handler, Lee Cox, received the Kendall Memorial Trophy and a £200 cash prize.

item.title: Pitch Invasion by Celtic and Rangers Fans Condemned by Police
item.description: Celtic and Rangers fans clashed during a pitch invasion after an Old Firm match in Glasgow, resulting in injuries and arrests.

item.title: Mapped: US-Israeli Strikes Across Iran and the Middle East
item.description: US-Israeli strikes have continued across Iran and the Middle East, with reports of oil depots being hit in Tehran and civilian casualties mounting. The Israeli military has attacked 400 targets in western and central Iran.
